name: keeping-the-moon
description: Sarah Dessen's young adult novel about fifteen-year-old Colie Sparks, a former fat girl sent to spend the summer with her eccentric aunt in a beach town, where she learns about friendship, self-acceptance, and finding her own light.
useCase: A coming-of-age story about body image, self-esteem, and the transformative power of friendship — for teens and anyone who has ever felt invisible.
knownRisks: The novel deals with body image issues and weight that may be triggering for readers with eating disorders.
Published in 1999; some cultural references (aerobic workout videos, landlines) are dated.
The weight loss narrative has been critiqued by modern readers as problematic.
